title: Student cannot start a second course while one is active
preconditions: A student account exists with id student-1
student-1 has an active course with id course-100
steps: Send POST /courses with a valid new course payload
Capture the HTTP response status and body
expected_result: The API returns HTTP 409 and an error indicating the student already has an active course. No new course record is created in the database.

title: Student can start a course when no active course exists
preconditions: A student account exists with id student-2
student-2 has no active course
steps: Send POST /courses with a valid new course payload
Capture the HTTP response status and body
expected_result: The API returns HTTP 201 and the response body contains the new course id with status active.

title: Student with only an archived course can start a new course
preconditions: A student account exists with id student-3
student-3 has exactly one course with status archived
steps: Send POST /courses with a valid new course payload
Capture the HTTP response status and body
expected_result: The API returns HTTP 201. The new course is created with status active. The pre-existing archived course remains archived.

title: Admin receives a listing of all courses with their statuses
preconditions: An admin account exists
The database contains courses owned by multiple students with statuses active and archived
expected_result: The API returns HTTP 200 with an array containing every course in the database. Each entry includes course id, owner student id, and current status.

title: Non-admin student is rejected from the admin course listing endpoint
preconditions: A student account exists with id student-4
expected_result: The API returns HTTP 403. No course data is included in the response body.

title: Admin course listing returns empty array when no courses exist
preconditions: An admin account exists
The database contains no courses
expected_result: The API returns HTTP 200 with an empty array. No error is returned.

title: Withdrawing from an active course sets its status to archived
preconditions: A student account exists with id student-5
student-5 has an active course with id course-200
steps: Query the database for the status of course-200
expected_result: The API returns HTTP 200. The database record for course-200 has status archived.

title: Attempting to withdraw from a course not owned by the student is rejected
preconditions: Student accounts student-5 and student-6 exist
student-5 owns active course course-200
student-6 is authenticated
expected_result: The API returns HTTP 403. The status of course-200 remains active in the database.

title: Withdrawing from an already-archived course is a no-op rejection
preconditions: A student account exists with id student-7
student-7 has an archived course with id course-300
expected_result: The API returns HTTP 409 indicating the course is not active. The database record for course-300 remains archived.

title: Student can resume an archived course, restoring it to active
preconditions: A student account exists with id student-8
student-8 has an archived course with id course-400 and no active course
steps: Query the database for the status of course-400
expected_result: The API returns HTTP 200. The database record for course-400 has status active.

title: Student with an active course cannot resume a second archived course
preconditions: A student account exists with id student-9
student-9 has an active course course-500 and an archived course course-501
expected_result: The API returns HTTP 409 indicating the student already has an active course. course-501 remains archived in the database.

title: Resuming an active course is rejected
preconditions: A student account exists with id student-10
student-10 has an active course with id course-600
expected_result: The API returns HTTP 409. The status of course-600 remains active in the database.

title: Student submits a syllabus modification request and it is persisted
preconditions: A student account exists with id student-11
student-11 has an active course course-700 with a generated syllabus awaiting approval
steps: Send POST /courses/course-700/syllabus/modification-request with body containing requested changes
Query the database for modification requests associated with course-700
expected_result: The API returns HTTP 201. The database contains a modification request record linked to course-700 with the submitted change details and a pending status.

title: Syllabus modification request with empty body is rejected
preconditions: A student account exists with id student-11
student-11 has an active course course-700 with a generated syllabus awaiting approval
steps: Send POST /courses/course-700/syllabus/modification-request with an empty body
expected_result: The API returns HTTP 400. No modification request record is created in the database.

title: Content generation is blocked when syllabus is not yet approved
preconditions: A student account exists with id student-12
student-12 has an active course course-800 with a generated syllabus in pending-approval state
expected_result: The API returns HTTP 409 indicating the syllabus has not been approved. No content generation job is created or started.

title: Content generation proceeds after student approves syllabus
preconditions: A student account exists with id student-13
student-13 has an active course course-900 with a syllabus that has been approved
steps: Capture the HTTP response status and job identifier
expected_result: The API returns HTTP 202 and a job identifier. A content generation job record is created in the database for course-900.

title: Approved syllabus due dates are stored in the database
preconditions: A student account exists with id student-14
student-14 has an active course course-1000 with an approved syllabus containing three homework items with distinct due dates
steps: Send POST /courses/course-1000/due-dates/confirm to finalize the schedule
Query the database for due date records linked to course-1000
expected_result: The database contains exactly three due date records for course-1000, each storing a homework id and an ISO-8601 date matching those defined in the syllabus.

title: Due dates are not persisted when syllabus has not been approved
preconditions: A student account exists with id student-15
student-15 has an active course course-1100 with a syllabus in pending-approval state
expected_result: The API returns HTTP 409. No due date records are created in the database for course-1100.

title: Student explicitly agrees to due date schedule before finalization
preconditions: A student account exists with id student-16
student-16 has an active course course-1200 with an approved syllabus and a proposed due date schedule
steps: Send POST /courses/course-1200/due-dates/agree with body agreeing to the schedule
Query the database for the agreement record on course-1200
expected_result: The API returns HTTP 200. The database records that student-16 has explicitly agreed to the due date schedule for course-1200, and the schedule status is finalized.

title: Due date schedule is not finalized without student agreement
preconditions: A student account exists with id student-17
student-17 has an active course course-1300 with an approved syllabus and a proposed due date schedule with no agreement recorded
steps: Attempt to submit homework for course-1300 without having agreed to the schedule
expected_result: The API returns HTTP 409 indicating the due date schedule has not been agreed to. No submission is accepted.
